story_lead: Iron Lace began with a twenty-year friendship and a shared dream — that a good book, a quiet chair, and a word of grace can meet a person right where they are.
announcement: Closed July 3rd and July 4th for Independence Day
story_seal_note: Proudly run with the discipline, heart, and service learned in the Corps.

reading.title: The Indigo Girl
reading.author: Natasha Boyd
reading.blurb: In 1739, sixteen-year-old Eliza Lucas Pinckney is left in charge of her family’s three South Carolina plantations while her father pursues military ambitions abroad. With the family’s future in jeopardy, Eliza becomes determined to cultivate indigo, a valuable blue dye crop that many believe cannot be successfully produced in the colony. Facing opposition from family, social expectations, and the realities of plantation life, she forms unlikely alliances and risks everything to achieve what others say is impossible. Based on historical letters and documents, the novel tells the story of the young woman whose innovation helped transform South Carolina’s economy.

story_body:
Best friends for more than two decades, **Laurie Bowen Jacobs** — a former **United States Marine** and mother of five — and **Tiffany Addis Murr** mother of five and Nana of five had long dreamed of opening a bookstore together. When a beloved Anderson bookshop closed its doors after **47 years**, they knew the time had come — and on **December 1, 2021**, Iron Lace opened its own. The warm framework around you was built by Laurie’s husband from reclaimed and burnt-finish timber, beam by beam, with the same patience they bring to every reader who wanders in.

Inside you'll find aisles of gently-loved books at **half the cover price**, faith and fiction shoulder to shoulder. Bring in your own gently-used paperbacks and we'll credit you **a quarter of the cover price** in store credit — good toward up to half of any purchase. Pull up a chair, and stay awhile.

menu_coffee.name: The Daily Bread Coffee
menu_coffee.description: Our freshly brewed house blend — simple, dependable, and always there when you need it. Just like daily grace. Bottomless refills available.
menu_coffee.verse: “Give us this day our daily bread.” — Matt. 6:11

menu_coffee.name: Living Water Latte
menu_coffee.description: Single or double shot of espresso, steamed milk, and a thick, velvety crown of foam. Bold beneath and light on top — just as we are called to be
menu_coffee.verse: “Whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.” — John 4:14

menu_coffee.name: The Morning Manna Latte
menu_coffee.description: Single or double shot of espresso, steamed milk, with maple and brown sugar and a thick, velvety crown of foam with cinnamon sprinkled on top. Bold beneath and light on top — just as we are called to be
menu_coffee.verse: "Because of the Lord's great love we are not consumed, for his compassions never fail.  They are new every morning; great is his faithfulness"- Lam.3:22-23

menu_coffee.name: The Garden Mocha
menu_coffee.description: Espresso with rich chocolate or white chocolate, steamed milk, and a swirl of whipped cream and caramel. A reminder that even in the darkest hour, something sweet is waiting.
menu_coffee.verse: " Weeping may stay for the night,but joy comes in the morning"- Psalms 30:5

menu_coffee.name: The Emmaus Latte
menu_coffee.real_name: Honey Vanilla Latte with a sprinkle of Cinnamon
menu_coffee.description: Single or double shot of espresso, steamed milk, with honey and vanilla and a thick, velvety crown of foam with cinnamon sprinkled on top. Bold beneath and light on top — just as we are called to be
menu_coffee.verse: " Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?" Luke 24:32

menu_tea.name: Morning Mercies
menu_tea.description: A rich, malty blend to start your day anchored in gratitude. Served with honey and your choice of milk — simple, dependable, and deeply comforting.
menu_tea.verse: “His mercies are new every morning.” — Lam. 3:23

menu_tea.name: The Anointed Cup
menu_tea.description: Classic black tea lifted with bergamot's bright citrus note — elegant, fragrant, and like a moment set apart. Wonderful hot or iced.
menu_tea.verse: “You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.” — Ps. 23:5

menu_tea.name: Armor of God Chai
menu_tea.description: Bold black tea with cinnamon, cardamom, ginger, cloves, and pepper — as strong and layered as the armor we put on each day. Steamed with oat or whole milk.
menu_tea.verse: “Put on the full armor of God.” — Eph. 6:11

menu_tea.name: Fruit of the Spirit
menu_tea.description: A radiant blend of hibiscus, rosehip, and mixed berries — tart, bright, and full of life. Gorgeous hot or iced with a sprig of mint.
menu_tea.verse: “The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace…” — Gal. 5:22

menu_tea.name: Light of the World Matcha
menu_tea.description: Vibrant, earthy ceremonial matcha whisked with steamed milk and a whisper of honey. Bold in color, rich in flavor — a cup as bright as the light we carry.
menu_tea.verse: “You are the light of the world.” — Matt. 5:14

picks.title: Rooms
picks.author: James Rubart
picks.blurb: On a rainy spring day in Seattle, young software tycoon Micah Taylor receives a cryptic twenty-five-year-old letter from a great-uncle he never knew. The letter claims a home awaits him on the Oregon coast that will turn his world upside down. Suspecting a prank, Micah arrives to discover a stunning new house. But when bizarre things begin happening in its rooms, he learns the home is far more than a house—it is a physical manifestation of his soul. As Micah confronts painful truths from his past, God uses the house to heal his deepest wounds and lead him toward an astonishing new destiny.

picks.title: Edge-of-Your-Seat Reads
picks.author: Woman Down by Colleen Hoover
picks.blurb: In this twisty thriller, frustrated author Petra Rose retreats to a remote cabin, hoping to overcome her writer’s block and save her career. But when a mysterious police officer named Saint appears at her door investigating an accident nearby, Petra finds the inspiration she’s been desperately seeking. As their relationship deepens, strange events begin to blur the line between reality and fiction, forcing Petra to question everything she believes about her life, her work, and the people around her.
